retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Joints -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: A joint is a region where 2 bones make contact. Joints may be classified histologically or functionally. Histological classification is based on the predominant connective tissue type composing the joint, either fibrous, cartilaginous, or synovial. Functional classification is based on the amount of movement the joint permits. The 3 functional joint types include the immovable synarthrosis, slightly moveable amphiarthrosis, and freely moveable diarthrosis. The 2 joint classification schemes correlate: synarthroses are fibrous, amphiarthroses are cartilaginous, and diarthroses are synovial. [1] [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Triple Arthrodesis -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the anatomical structures, indications, and contraindications of the triple arthrodesis. Describe the equipment, personnel, preparation, and technique in regards to the triple arthrodesis. Review the potential complications and clinical significance of the triple arthrodesis. Summarize interprofessional team strategies for improving care coordination and communication to advance the triple arthrodesis and improve outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Joints -- Structure and Function -- Fibrous Joints
retrieved_docs.content: A fibrous joint is a fixed joint (synarthrosis) where collagenous fibrous connective tissue unites 2 bones. Fibrous joints are usually immovable and lack a joint cavity. These joints are further classified into sutures, gomphoses, and syndesmoses.

retrieved_docs.title: Dysarthria -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Dysarthria is described as chronic if persistent for greater than 5 years. Dysarthia is considered stable in patients with nonprogressive etiologies.

retrieved_docs.title: Dysarthria -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to the neurological causes, nonneurological causes, such as cleft lip or palate and laryngeal tumors, also cause difficulty with articulation. However, the condition is not termed dysarthria if it stems from nonneurological causes.

retrieved_docs.title: Arthritis -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Arthritis is a broad and complex topic with many types, subtypes, and variations. The above is a summary of this expansive subject and does not aim to cover it in its entirety. It does, however, aim to provide an introductory synopsis of the arthritides and give the reader the knowledge to diagnose and treat this condition in most settings.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Joints -- Structure and Function -- Cartilaginous Joints
retrieved_docs.content: A permanent synchondrosis does not ossify with age but retains its hyaline cartilage. Permanent synchondroses connect bones without allowing any movement, similar to a synarthrosis. These joints are commonly found in the thoracic cage. For instance, the 1st sternocostal joint unites the 1st rib to the manubrium by its costal cartilage. Other examples include the connections between the anterior ends of the remaining 11 ribs and their respective costal cartilages. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Triple Arthrodesis -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The triple arthrodesis is considered a definitive procedure for many pedal deformities and gait disturbances.  Conditions it may be performed in include [6] : End-stage flatfoot deformities due to loss of soft tissue structures or joint adaptive changes of surrounding joints and pain Degenerative or Posttraumatic Arthritis due to pain Cavus foot deformities due to joint adaptive changes of surrounding joints and pain Charcot arthropathy after coalescence with risk for ulceration, or an apropulsive gait. Tarsal coalitions due to pain or degeneration of the ankle joint/surrounding joints Residual clubfoot deformities causing pain, joint degenerative disease or an apropulsive gait Certain neurological conditions resulting in neuromuscular imbalances and an apropulsive gait [7] [1] [8] : Charcot-Marie-Tooth Poliomyelitis Friedreich ataxia Spina bifida Cerebral palsy

retrieved_docs.title: Triple Arthrodesis -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Many conditions may be treated using other motion-preserving procedures, so the decision to perform the triple arthrodesis is always dependant on patient condition, disease progression, and surgeon preference.

retrieved_docs.title: Radioulnar Synostosis -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: While radioulnar synostosis may present at any age, children with congenital radioulnar synostosis present around age 6 years, when tasks or activities of daily living requiring forearm rotation are limited due to an inability to achieve full pronation or supination. Affected children may have difficulty turning door knobs or throwing a ball. However, the anatomical level of the fusion determines which activities are most negatively affected. For example, children with a fixed pronation deformity may have difficulty with tasks requiring supination, such as washing their face, brushing their teeth, eating, or catching a ball. In contrast, children with a fixed supination deformity will have difficulty with tasks requiring pronation, such as typing, writing, or other tabletop activities requiring the palms to face downward. Patients may also demonstrate a block to elbow flexion or extension if the synostosis extends proximally or involves abnormal positioning of the radial head.

retrieved_docs.title: Wrist Arthrodesis -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: There are no specific contraindications for this procedure. Patient refusal to have the procedure as well as an allergic reaction to the implant material can be considered contraindications. In certain circumstances, some patients might benefit from alternative procedures such as full joint replacement rather than wrist fusion. The decision regarding surgical procedure needs to be discussed between the patient and the surgical consultant depending on their age, functional requirements, and the condition affecting the wrist.

retrieved_docs.title: Dysarthria -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In the neuromuscular junction, acetylcholine produced in the presynaptic nerve terminal binds to receptors, ultimately creating an endplate potential strong enough to propagate action potential over the surface of the skeletal muscle membrane, resulting in muscle contraction. In myasthenia gravis, various autoantibodies interrupt these processes resulting in dysarthria and other symptoms. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Wrist Instability -- Etiology -- Neurological
retrieved_docs.content: Usually through the development of neurogenic arthropathies, i.e., Charcot-like joints Syringomyelia [4]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1988
retrieved_docs.content: Other OR therapeutic procedures on joints are classified as other operations on joint structures, indicating that these therapeutic interventions are grouped within the broader category of joint operations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1853
retrieved_docs.content: Other OR therapeutic procedures on joints is a classification that categorizes other or therapeutic procedures on joints.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1919
retrieved_docs.content: Arthrodesis of the elbow is a surgical procedure that falls within the scope of Orthopaedic Surgery (physician group), indicating that it is performed by orthopaedic surgeons who specialize in musculoskeletal interventions. This procedure involves fusing the elbow joint to provide stability and pain relief in cases where other treatments are insufficient, and its classification under orthopaedic surgery underscores its relevance to the specialty’s focus on bone and joint disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 1797
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Other OR therapeutic procedures on joints” serves as a classification label for the category “other or therapeutic procedures on joints,” and it is itself classified under the broader group “Other operations on joint structures.” This hierarchical relationship indicates that the specific set of other or therapeutic joint procedures is organized within the larger classification of joint‑structure operations, providing a structured framework for coding and understanding joint‑related therapeutic interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 143
retrieved_docs.content: Arthropathy associated with an endocrine AND/OR metabolic disorder is classified as other non‑traumatic joint disorders and its primary finding site is the joints (klouby). Other non‑traumatic joint disorders are linked to the concept of اعتلال مفصلي (joint disease). In the context of surgical procedures, Division of joint capsule, ligament, or cartilage is a subcategory (has parent) of Incision and excision of joint structures and it classifies the anatomical category “division of joint capsule; ligament or cartilage.” Incision and excision of joint structures also has a relationship with the joints (klouby). This integrated knowledge connects endocrine‑related arthropathy to non‑traumatic joint pathology, emphasizes its joint involvement, and outlines the hierarchical relationship between joint‑focused surgical interventions and the specific joint structures they address.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 1184
retrieved_docs.content: effects as specific conditions associated with the concept of “كسر,” situates them within a broader category of unspecified injury sequelae, and further delineates limb‑specific fracture sequelae, thereby providing a structured framework for understanding and managing the long‑term consequences of various bone injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 2 -- Community 1174
retrieved_docs.content: shared therapeutic goals for musculoskeletal and connective‑tissue conditions. This integrated framework supports comprehensive patient care by aligning specific modalities with appropriate clinical indications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2851
retrieved_docs.content: Other non‑traumatic joint disorders are closely associated with joint disease (اعتلال مفصلي), indicating that a range of non‑injury‑related joint conditions fall under the broader category of joint disease. This relationship highlights the importance of recognizing various non‑traumatic joint pathologies as part of the overall spectrum of اعتلال مفصلي for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 2221
retrieved_docs.content: Arthrodesis of elbow is classified as an “Other OR therapeutic procedure on joints,” a category that itself is classified as “Other operations on joint structures.” This surgical joint fusion procedure falls within the domain of Orthopaedic Surgery (physician group), indicating that it is typically performed by orthopaedic surgeons who specialize in operative management of joint conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 1 -- Community 128
retrieved_docs.content: spectrum, emphasizing their central role in entrapment syndromes, metabolic and inflammatory neuropathies, localized nerve lesions, musculoskeletal deformities, and broader movement disorders. This interconnected framework supports comprehensive documentation, precise diagnosis, and interdisciplinary communication for patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 1 -- Community 181
retrieved_docs.content: diseases, genetic neuropathies, and distinct syndromes. This unified structure supports coding, diagnosis, and patient‑centered management of a broad array of neurological conditions, emphasizing the central role of peripheral nerves, pain, paralysis, and targeted surgical decompression in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1921
retrieved_docs.content: Arthrotomy with removal of prosthesis is a specific therapeutic intervention performed on joints. It is categorized under the broader group of other or therapeutic procedures on joints, indicating that it is one of several surgical options aimed at treating joint pathology. Additionally, Arthrotomy with removal of prosthesis is identified as a type of بضع المفصل, linking it to the general concept of joint removal procedures. This classification highlights its role within the spectrum of joint surgeries and underscores its relevance for clinicians selecting appropriate operative strategies for prosthetic complications or severe joint disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 1 -- Community 242
retrieved_docs.content: joint incision and excision. Overall, these triples illustrate a structured taxonomy of joint‑related surgical interventions, linking specific procedures such as arthrodesis, arthroscopy, arthrotomy with prosthesis removal, and capsule/ligament division to their appropriate procedural classifications and the surgical specialties that perform them. This integrated framework supports clear communication among clinicians, accurate coding, and coordinated patient care for a wide range of joint pathologies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2675
retrieved_docs.content: Symptoms are closely related to, and may be considered synonymous with, the signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, reflecting an overlap in terminology where both terms describe the observable manifestations and patient‑reported experiences associated with poorly characterized medical disorders. This conceptual equivalence highlights the importance of recognizing that “symptoms” and “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions” often refer to the same clinical features, facilitating clearer communication in diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 779
retrieved_docs.content: Arthrodesis of elbow is classified as an “Other OR therapeutic procedure on joints,” placing it within the broader category of “Other operations on joint structures.” This procedure is performed by physicians specializing in Orthopaedic Surgery, highlighting its relevance to the orthopaedic specialty. Similarly, the “Arthroscopic procedure of joint, not elsewhere classified” is primarily mapped to the same overarching group of “Other operations on joint structures” and is directly associated with arthroscopy, indicating that it represents a specific arthroscopic technique not captured elsewhere. Together, these relationships illustrate how both arthrodesis of the elbow and non‑specified arthroscopic joint procedures are organized under a common classification of joint operations, reflecting their shared focus on therapeutic interventions of joint structures within orthopaedic practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 917
retrieved_docs.content: Arthropathy associated with an endocrine and/or metabolic disorder is a musculoskeletal condition whose findings are located in the klouby (joints).
